boundary: This is a source-refresh and experiment-routing packet. It does not prove SAIR claims, reproduce challenge results, or assert market uniqueness.

channel_leads.title: Terence Tao: What's Next for SAIR Competitions
A Conversation with Yannis Ioannidis, ACM's Longest-Serving President
Alex Oshmyansky: How Cost Plus Drugs Is Rebuilding Prescription Drug Pricing
Modular Arithmetic Challenge: SAIR Playground Guide
Title: Panel Discussion: AI x Open Data
Carina Hong: Frontiers of AI for Mathematical Research
Compression is all you need: Modeling mathematics
Tom Kalil: Funding AI for Science Through Public Goods and Philanthropy
David Patterson & Andy Konwinski: How to Accelerate Frontier Science
Panel Discussion - AI x Life Science

experiments: Capture competition Markdown shadows and GitHub repositories for IGP24, modular arithmetic, Stage 1, and Stage 2.
Build a SAIR API adapter only after official API docs or a stable endpoint contract is captured.
Install Lean/lake/elan or containerized Lean replay so Stage 2 certificates move from preflight to executable replay.
Prototype BuildLang/buildc exact-arithmetic kernels against modular arithmetic tasks with cost and correctness receipts.
Create an inverse-Galois packet schema joining polynomial candidates, CAS commands, source references, and review verdicts.
Convert the Tao competition transcript into timestamped claim cards and reject all claims not cross-checked to official pages.
Score SAIR-adjacent market wedges against research labs, enterprise AI/R&D, competition hosts, and benchmark providers.
Promote only experiments backed by current Gather, Index, Forum, Crucible, and Telos receipts.

megatool_routes.market_gap_hypothesis: Competition infrastructure proves narrow challenge outputs but rarely packages source, solver, formal certificate, runtime receipt, and market-facing explanation as one reusable object.
megatool_routes.output: claim-to-proof packets for math competitions with prompt, solver, certificate, judge, and replay receipts

megatool_routes.market_gap_hypothesis: AI benchmark work needs harsher exact-compute gates that expose where neural methods fail arithmetic structure.
megatool_routes.output: large-integer modular arithmetic benchmark receipts with correctness, cost, and failure-mode envelopes

megatool_routes.market_gap_hypothesis: Mathematical discovery contests need reproducible computation trails that join literature, CAS state, and proof obligations.
megatool_routes.output: candidate polynomial, group, source, computation, and reviewer packet workflow

megatool_routes.market_gap_hypothesis: Public competition APIs can become reusable proof-packet intake surfaces when calls are admitted and replayed with receipts.
megatool_routes.megatool: Gather + Telos action receipts + Index adapters
megatool_routes.output: typed adapter spec for SAIR benchmark/playground/task surfaces

megatool_routes.market_gap_hypothesis: The buyer motion is not one tool sale; it is a proof-centered collaboration substrate spanning scientists, enterprise data, compute, and results.
megatool_routes.output: buyer/problem map for frontier labs, enterprise R&D, philanthropy, and AI-for-science partnerships

megatool_routes.market_gap_hypothesis: Video channels encode fast-moving expert context that should become testable labs, not summaries.
megatool_routes.output: source-backed concept cards, lab prompts, transcript claim cards, and negative fixtures

negative_fixtures: Empty static Gather capture must not be interpreted as an empty page.
Auto-caption transcript must not be treated as exact speech without timestamp review.
A video title is a source lead, not a verified technical claim.
Channel flat-list upload_date NA must not become a guessed publication date.
Official challenge description must not imply our solver passes or has leaderboard standing.
SAIR API video title must not imply an API contract has been captured.
BuildLang/buildc route must not be treated as an implemented adapter until runnable tasks exist.
No theorem, natural law, benchmark win, or market uniqueness claim is promoted by this pass.

Terence Tao: What's Next for SAIR Competitions

source_scope: SAIR Foundation official site, competition llms.txt shadows, supplied YouTube video, SAIR channel flat-list fixture
updated_tool_experiments: Use Gather 1.5.0 sequential video intake for all 12 channel leads; avoid parallel writes to one corpus.
Use Index 2.8.0 atlas and select receipts to build budgeted SAIR competition context packs.
Use Forum 1.12.0 routing to split SAIR work into proof, exact-compute, science-network, market, and Learning Forge lanes.
Use Crucible 1.1.0 run/recheck to gate each claim, route, and negative fixture before promotion.
Use Telos 0.1.0 Learning Forge Labs and Model Foundry surfaces for runnable lab contracts and bounded model/tool loops.
Use Telos action receipts/context packs to join source intake, solver action, runtime output, and verifier verdict.

crucible.experiment_role: claim verdicts, negative fixtures, replayable assessment
crucible.surface: run, review, recheck, measurement-gate, batch, refine, verdicts

forum.experiment_role: route SAIR work into proof, science, benchmark, and market lanes
forum.surface: 28-lane roster, per-task context, model-foundry daemon routing

gather.experiment_role: source intake, corpus verification, transcript and metadata receipts
gather.surface: status, doctor, docs, web, feed, pdf, run, corpus, arxiv, video

index.experiment_role: large dogfood map, selection receipts, context envelopes
index.surface: workspace atlas, certificates, freshness, bench, select, context-envelope

telos.experiment_role: operator-room orchestration and megatool product surface
telos.surface: 65-tool catalog, Learning Forge Labs, Model Foundry, context packs, action receipts

yt_dlp.experiment_role: YouTube source-lead intake before claim promotion
yt_dlp.surface: video metadata, auto-captions, flat channel listing
